assignment.objective:
Do a comprehensive survey of all MVP/prototype hacks in the Rally project that need to be removed for production. Search for and report:

1. `backend/src/middleware/auth.ts` ‚Äî the NODE_ENV/development bypass on line 20
2. `frontend/Rally/src/store/slices/authSlice.ts` ‚Äî isAuthenticated: true hack

agents.result:
Good, I now have all the data needed. Let me compile the comprehensive report.

---

### SUMMARY
I performed a comprehensive survey of all 9 categories of MVP/prototype hacks in the Rally project. Every item requested was found and verified with file:line evidence. The project has widespread hardcoded localhost URLs (21 hits in frontend, 11 in backend), 33 files with `@ts-nocheck`, an auth bypass in middleware, a hardcoded `isAuthenticated: true` in Redux state, a notification signature mismatch, permissive CSP config, mock/fake data in production screens, and no `.env` `MVP_MODE` variable despite code checking for it.

### EVIDENCE

**1. Auth middleware NODE_ENV/MVP bypass**
- `backend/src/middleware/auth.ts:20` ‚Äî `if (process.env.MVP_MODE === 'true' || process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development')` skips all JWT auth, assigns guest user.
- `backend/.env:2` ‚Äî `NODE_ENV=development` is set; no `MVP_MODE` key exists at all, so the `MVP_MODE === 'true'` check is dead code.

**2. Redux isAuthenticated: true hack**
- `frontend/Rally/src/store/slices/authSlice.ts:41` ‚Äî `isAuthenticated: true, // MVP mode` in initial state. This means the app always believes the user is logged in, even without credentials.
- `frontend/Rally/src/navigation/AppNavigator.tsx:55-67` ‚Äî `RootNavigator` reads `isAuthenticated` from Redux and renders `Main` (authenticated) stack whenever true; the `registerPushToken` call at line 62 passes `(userId || '', deviceId || '')` instead of the single-argument signature `registerPushToken(deviceId: string)`.

**3. Hardcoded `http://localhost:3001` URLs**

| File | Line | Code |
|---|---|---|
| `backend/src/utils/fileUpload.ts` | 71 | `const baseUrl = process.env.API_BASE_URL \|\| 'http://localhost:3001'` |
| `backend/src/routes/shareCard.ts` | 44 | `const appUrl = process.env.FRONTEND_URL \|\| 'http://localhost:3001'` |
| `backend/src/routes/mvpSessions.ts` | 426 | ``shareLink: `${process.env.FRONTEND_URL \|\| 'http://localhost:3001'}/join/...` `` |
| `backend/src/routes/mvpSessions.ts` | 1000 | ``shareUrl: `${process.env.FRONTEND_URL \|\| 'http://localhost:3001'}/session/...` `` |
| `backend/src/services/oauthService.ts` | 26 | ``redirectUri: ... \|\| 'http://localhost:3001'/api/v1/oauth/google/callback`` |
| `backend/src/services/oauthService.ts` | 35 | Same pattern for WeChat callback |
| `backend/src/routes/webSession.ts` | 578 | `window.location.replace('http://localhost:3001' + ...)` ‚Äî bare string, no env fallback |
| `frontend/Rally/src/config/api.ts` | 6 | `(__DEV__ ? 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1' : '/api/v1')` ‚Äî `__DEV__` flag leak |
| `frontend/Rally/src/config.ts` | 10,13 | Returns `'http://localhost:3001'` via `Platform.OS` switching, no env-var escape |
| `frontend/Rally/src/services/apiService.ts` | 21 | `constructor(baseURL: string = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1')` |
| `frontend/Rally/src/services/apiClient.ts` | 2 | `const API_BASE = process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL \|\| 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` |
| `frontend/Rally/src/services/analyticsApi.ts` | 13 | `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` ‚Äî no env fallback |
| `frontend/Rally/src/services/NotificationService.ts` | 222, 251, 276 | Three separate `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` inline in methods |
| `frontend/Rally/src/services/mvpApiService.ts` | 65 | `super(process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL \|\| 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1')` |
| `frontend/Rally/src/hooks/useMessaging.ts` | 6 | `const SOCKET_URL = 'http://localhost:3001'` ‚Äî bare string, no env |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/PlayerProfileScreen.tsx` | 19 | `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/LiveGameScreen.tsx` | 31 | Same |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/JoinSessionScreen.tsx` | 17 | Same |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/CreateSessionScreen.tsx` | 78 | ``fetch(`http://localhost:3001/api/v1/session-templates/${deviceId}`)`` |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionSettingsScreen.tsx` | 20 | Same pattern |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx` | 27 | Same |
| `frontend/Rally/src/screens/LeaderboardScreen.tsx` | 14 | Same |
| `frontend/Rally/src/components/SessionSuggestions.tsx` | 43 | Same pattern |
| `frontend/Rally/src/components/ShareLinkHandler.tsx` | 56 | `const webFallbackUrl = 'http://localhost:3001/session/${shareCode}'` |
| `frontend/Rally/src/components/OrganizerClaimModal.tsx` | 24 | `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` |
| `backend/src/__tests__/validation.js` | 72 | Test file (lower priority) |
| `backend/src/__tests__/mvpSessions.basic.test.ts` | 99, 206 | Test files (lower priority) |
| `backend/src/__tests__/mvpSessions.unit.test.ts` | 181 | Test file (lower priority) |

**4. `@ts-nocheck` files (33 total)**
- `frontend/Rally/src/navigation/AppNavigator.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/store/index.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/FriendsListScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/pairing/PairingScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/PlayerProfileScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/LiveGameScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionHistoryScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/CreateSessionScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionSettingsScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/MySessionsScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionOverviewScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/rotation/RotationScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/UserProfileScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/TrendsDashboardScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDiscoveryScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/PredictionDashboardScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/TournamentAnalyticsScreen.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/SessionSuggestions.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/SessionFilters.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/design-system/Layout/SessionHeader.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/design-system/Layout/PlayerCountIndicator.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/hooks/useNotificationManager.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/NotificationService.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/mvpApiService.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/deviceService.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/realTimeService.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/apiService.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/notificationApi.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/theme/theme.ts:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/__tests__/QuickScoreRecordingScreen.test.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/hooks/__tests__/usePermissions.test.tsx:1`
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/__tests__/statisticsApi.test.ts:1`

**5. Notification init with old signature**
- `frontend/Rally/App.tsx:17` ‚Äî `await notificationService.registerPushToken(deviceId)` ‚Äî single argument.
- `frontend/Rally/src/navigation/AppNavigator.tsx:62` ‚Äî `await notificationService.registerPushToken(userId || '', deviceId || '')` ‚Äî **two** arguments, but the actual method signature at `NotificationService.ts:215` is `async registerPushToken(deviceId: string)` (one argument). The AppNavigator call is a **signature mismatch**.
- `frontend/Rally/src/hooks/useNotificationManager.ts:57,67` ‚Äî Defines its own local 2-arg `registerPushToken(pushToken, deviceId)` but calls `fetch` directly (not `notificationService.registerPushToken`) ‚Äî a **different implementation** of the same concept. Confusingly, the `/notifications/register` endpoint it hits at `frontend/Rally/src/services/notificationApi.ts:56` expects `RegisterPushTokenRequest: { token, platform, deviceId }`.

**6. Backend .env**
- `backend/.env:2` ‚Äî `NODE_ENV=development`
- `backend/.env` ‚Äî **no `MVP_MODE` key exists**. The auth middleware checks `process.env.MVP_MODE === 'true'` but the variable is not defined in `.env`. It relies entirely on `NODE_ENV === 'development'`.

**7. Frontend services with hardcoded localhost URLs (non-env-var)**
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/analyticsApi.ts:13` ‚Äî bare `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` with no env fallback
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/NotificationService.ts:222,251,276` ‚Äî three inline hardcoded `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` inside methods
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/apiService.ts:21` ‚Äî default parameter `'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` (but `apiClient.ts:2` uses `process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL` fallback)
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/mvpApiService.ts:65` ‚Äî has env fallback, so borderline acceptable
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/apiClient.ts:2` ‚Äî has env fallback, borderline
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/notificationApi.ts:3` ‚Äî imports `API_BASE_URL` from `../config/api`, which is the `__DEV__`-gated one

**8. TODO/FIXME/mock/fake references**
- **No `TODO` or `FIXME`** found in either backend/src or frontend/Rally/src (excluding test files).
- **`mock` in production code (non-test files):**
  - `frontend/Rally/src/screens/AchievementScreen.tsx:127` ‚Äî `// Mock player ID - in real app, get from auth context` + `const playerId = 'player-123'`
  - `frontend/Rally/src/screens/PlayerProfileScreen.tsx:127,131,175,185` ‚Äî `createMockProfile()` function used as fallback when API fails
  - `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionHistoryScreen.tsx:91,95,143,144` ‚Äî `// Mock current user`, `const currentUserName = 'Alice Chen'`, `// Mock calculation`
  - `frontend/Rally/src/hooks/useLocation.ts:89,95` ‚Äî `mockHasPermission`, `mockLocation` hardcoded coordinates (53.5444, -113.4909)
- **`fake` ‚Äî no matches** in either src tree.

**9. CSP/helmet config in server.ts**
- `backend/src/server.ts:31-42` ‚Äî helmet CSP directives:
  - `scriptSrc: ["'self'", "'unsafe-inline'", "'unsafe-eval'"]` ‚Äî allows inline scripts and `eval()`, which is permissive and defeats XSS protection. `scriptSrcAttr: ["'unsafe-inline'"]` also allows inline event handlers.
  - No `frame-ancestors` directive (clickjacking risk)
  - No `upgrade-insecure-requests` directive
  - No `report-uri` or `report-to` for CSP violation monitoring
  - CORS at lines 47-57 allows any `localhost:*` origin via regex `/^http:\/\/localhost:\d+$/` ‚Äî wide open in dev but dangerous if `NODE_ENV` accidentally left as development in production.

### CHANGES
None.

### RISKS

- **Auth bypass in production**: If `NODE_ENV` is not set to `production` (currently `development` in `.env`), every request gets a guest user. Mitigation: remove the entire `MVP_MODE`/`NODE_ENV` bypass block and set `NODE_ENV=production` in deployment.
- **CSP too permissive**: `'unsafe-eval'` and `'unsafe-inline'` script-src open the door to XSS. Mitigation: remove `'unsafe-eval'`, use nonces/hashes for inline scripts, add `frame-ancestors: 'none'`, and enforce HTTPS upgrade.
- **`@ts-nocheck` in 33 files**: Production code with type-checking disabled hides real bugs. Mitigation: remove each pragma and fix the underlying TypeScript errors.
- **`__DEV__` gate in `config/api.ts`**: This is a Metro/Expo global; if the app is built in release mode it falls through to `/api/v1` (relative), which is fine, but the `__DEV__` branch hardcodes localhost and is used by `notificationApi.ts`. Mitigation: remove the `__DEV__` ternary entirely, rely solely on `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL`.
- **Signature mismatch AppNavigator vs NotificationService**: `AppNavigator.tsx:62` calls `registerPushToken(userId, deviceId)` (2 args) but the method on `NotificationService.ts:215` takes only `(deviceId: string)`. This will silently pass `userId` as the deviceId and ignore the actual deviceId. Mitigation: unify to a single correct signature (the `notificationApi.ts` version expects `{ token, platform, deviceId }`).
- **Hardcoded `player-123` and `Alice Chen` in production screens**: AchievementScreen and SessionHistoryScreen use mock data as fallback logic. This produces incorrect UX in production. Mitigation: wire up real auth context and remove mock fallbacks.

### BLOCKERS
None.

Do a thorough code review of the Rally project backend. Read and analyze these areas:

agents.result:
Now I have a thorough understanding of all the code. Let me compile the review.

---

### SUMMARY

I reviewed 7 backend files across routes and services in the Rally project. The review found 2 **BLOCKER** issues (data corruption on game deletion, no-transaction club creation), 8 **MAJOR** issues (missing auth on check-in endpoints, stale winRate after deletion, parallel statistics system, fake ELO/form data, broken head-to-head win counting, missing player-not-found handling in percentiles, PER_PLAYER cost model relying on missing field, and weekly decay corrupting denormalized data), and numerous **MINOR** issues covering code quality, consistency, and edge-case handling. The DELETE game endpoint is the most dangerous ‚Äî it reverts raw counters but never recalculates derived metrics (winRate, partnershipStats, playTime, etc.), leaving player data permanently inconsistent.

### EVIDENCE

- `backend/src/routes/mvpSessions.ts:1920-1942` ‚Äî DELETE game reverts `gamesPlayed`, `wins`, `losses` via raw `decrement` but never calls `recalculatePlayerMetrics` or reverts partnership stats, totalSetsWon, totalPlayTime.
- `backend/src/routes/mvpSessions.ts:1920` ‚Äî Player lookup uses `name: { in: allPlayers }` which is ambiguous when multiple players share a name in a session.
- `backend/src/routes/mvpSessions.ts:3372-3418` ‚Äî check-in endpoint has no `requireOrganizer` middleware; any unauthenticated caller can check in any player.
- `backend/src/routes/mvpSessions.ts:3430-3480` ‚Äî check-out endpoint likewise lacks authorization.
- `backend/src/services/clubService.ts:30-48` ‚Äî `createClub` performs `club.create` then `clubMember.create` sequentially without a Prisma transaction; member creation failure orphans the club.
- `backend/src/services/costSharingService.ts:75` ‚Äî `PER_PLAYER` branch reads `(session as any).costPerPlayer` ‚Äî `costPerPlayer` lives on `SessionConfig`, not `mvpSession`.
- `backend/src/services/smartInsightsService.ts:66-68` ‚Äî ELO derived from win rate + ad-hoc log formula; not from actual game results. Comment on line 70 admits this.
- `backend/src/services/smartInsightsService.ts:70-76` ‚Äî Recent form fabricated via `for` loop over win rate rather than pulled from real game history. Comment admits this.
- `backend/src/services/smartInsightsService.ts:104-116` ‚Äî `findMostBalancedTeams` only considers 4 pre-selected players (lowest, highest, two middle) even when more than 4 are available; ignores other valid combinations.
- `backend/src/services/rankingService.ts:89-100` ‚Äî `getGlobalRankings` deduplicates by keeping the *first* player found (arbitrary DB order), discarding potentially better stats from later records.
- `backend/src/services/rankingService.ts:122-137` ‚Äî `applyWeeklyDecay` mutates `winRate` directly without adjusting `wins` or `gamesPlayed`, making the three fields inconsistent.
- `backend/src/services/statisticsService.ts:1-599` vs `backend/src/utils/statisticsService.ts:1-400+` ‚Äî Two separate statistics service implementations; routes use both, creating architectural split.
- `backend/src/services/statisticsService.ts:519-560` ‚Äî `getHeadToHead` counts wins only from `matches.slice(0, 5)` but `totalMatches` returns the full count, creating a mismatch.
- `backend/src/services/statisticsService.ts:502-504` ‚Äî `getPlayerPercentiles` throws `new Error('Player not found')` instead of returning a null/empty result, causing route to return 500 for a 404 case.
- `backend/src/routes/statistics.ts:15-38` ‚Äî `player/:playerId` route has no `param` validation, no auth.
- `backend/src/routes/statistics.ts:75-98` ‚Äî `leaderboard` route allows unbounded result sets (limit only applied post-query, but underlying `getLeaderboard` doesn't support limit).
- `backend/src/routes/statistics.ts:190-210` ‚Äî All new statistics routes lack auth middleware; any caller can fetch any player's stats.

### CHANGES

None.

### RISKS

- **Data integrity regression after game deletion**: Since `recalculatePlayerMetrics` is not called, players' `winRate`, `matchWinRate`, `averageGameDuration`, `partnershipStats`, `totalSetsWon`, `totalSetsLost`, and `totalPlayTime` become permanently stale after a game is deleted. Mitigation: call the existing `recalculatePlayerMetrics` for all affected players and add partnership stat reversal logic.
- **Denormalized rating corruption from weekly decay**: `applyWeeklyDecay` changes `winRate` without changing `wins`/`gamesPlayed`, so any code that re-derives winRate from those fields will silently undo the decay ‚Äî or worse, produce inconsistent data. Mitigation: either derive rating from immutable data, or adjust `wins`/`losses` alongside the decay.
- **Two statistics services diverge**: `services/statisticsService.ts` uses the new `Match` model; `utils/statisticsService.ts` uses `mvpGame`. Routes and consumers mix them, leading to different stats depending on which path is hit. Mitigation: consolidate into one service and migrate all consumers.
- **No auth on stats/check-in endpoints**: All new routes are publicly callable. Any device can check in/check out players, view any player's streaks/percentiles/head-to-head. Mitigation: add `requireOrganizer` or at minimum `requireOrganizerOrSelf` middleware.

### BLOCKERS

None.

Do a thorough code review of the Rally project frontend. Read and analyze these areas:

1. `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx` ‚Äî the largest screen (~93K). Check for: the recently added Recent Games section, check-in UI, player search/filter, pairing button. Look for: excessive re-renders, missing error states, performance issues.

2. `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionSettingsScreen.tsx` ‚Äî new screen from our session. Check for: all form controls, save logic, preset handling, error states.

3. `frontend/Rally/src/screens/pairing/PairingScreen.tsx` ‚Äî rewritten pairing screen. Check for: algorithm selector, manual pairing logic, state management, stale closure bugs.

4. `frontend/Rally/src/screens/TrendsDashboardScreen.tsx` ‚Äî new 5-tab analytics. Check for: chart rendering, data fetching patterns, empty states, tab switching performance.

For each file, report: issues found (critical/high/medium/low), code quality observations, and specific recommendations.

agents.result:
Now I have all the evidence needed. Let me compile the comprehensive report.

---

### SUMMARY

Reviewed 8 files across the Rally frontend (~175K chars total). Found **1 BLOCKER** (runtime crash in SessionShareModal from missing `Linking` import), **14 MAJOR** issues spanning stale-closure bugs, missing error states, performance anti-patterns, silent failures, and dead code, and **5 MINOR** issues. The `SessionDetailScreen` at 3,066 lines is the single largest risk surface: 21 `useState` calls, inline filter/sort per render, hardcoded API URLs bypassing service wrappers, and a `process.env` reference that silently resolves to `undefined` in React Native.

---

### EVIDENCE

- `frontend/Rally/src/components/SessionShareModal.tsx:1-8` ‚Äî imports React, RN components, Ionicons, QRCode; **no import of `Linking`** despite `Linking.openURL()` call at line 127
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/SessionShareModal.tsx:127` ‚Äî `Linking.openURL(waUrl).catch(...)` ‚Äî will throw `ReferenceError: Can't find variable: Linking` at runtime
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx:1` ‚Äî `// @ts-nocheck` disables all TypeScript checking for a 3,066-line file
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx:27` ‚Äî `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1';` ‚Äî duplicates `config/api.ts`; 7 screens do this
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx:155-160` ‚Äî three filter/sort state variables (`playerSearch`, `playerStatusFilter`, `playerSort`); 21 total `useState` calls
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx:256-278` ‚Äî `initializeScreen` calls `await fetchSessionData(...)` then immediately reads `sessionData` state (line 275: `if (params.isNewSession && sessionData)`) ‚Äî **stale closure**: `sessionData` is still the pre-fetch value
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx:768,809` ‚Äî `process.env.FRONTEND_URL` used in template literals; React Native doesn't expose `process.env` without babel plugin ‚Üí always `'http://localhost:3000'` fallback, silently broken
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx:648-650` ‚Äî score validation: `scoreForm.team1FinalScore < 0 || scoreForm.team1FinalScore > 2` ‚Äî **caps scores at 2**; real badminton scoring is to 21
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx:2407,2673` ‚Äî `gameActions` style key **defined twice** in `StyleSheet.create`; second definition silently overwrites first
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx:1180-1200` ‚Äî player list inline `.filter().filter().sort().map()` chain executed every render; no `useMemo`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionDetailScreen.tsx:467,477` ‚Äî `handleCheckIn(playerId, playerName)` and `handleCheckOut(playerId, playerName)` accept `playerName` parameter but **never use it**
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionSettingsScreen.tsx:456-468` ‚Äî `Alert.prompt` used for template save; `Alert.prompt` **does not exist** in React Native (iOS-only, removed) ‚Üí the condition `Alert.prompt ? ...` is always falsy ‚Üí template save is **dead code**
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/SessionSettingsScreen.tsx:20` ‚Äî hardcodes `API_BASE_URL` instead of importing from `config/api`
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/pairing/PairingScreen.tsx:101-113` ‚Äî `handleAddToCourt` hardcodes `p.players.length < 2` (max 2 per court); ignores court configuration
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/TrendsDashboardScreen.tsx:49-70` ‚Äî `loadAllData` fetches all 5 tabs' data on mount via `Promise.all` regardless of which tab is active; loading state never resolves if `playerId` is undefined (lines 52-59 skipped, but `setLoading(true)` already called)
- `frontend/Rally/src/components/SessionSuggestions.tsx:43-45` ‚Äî returns `null` when no suggestions, causing potential layout shift in parent
- `frontend/Rally/src/screens/CreateSessionScreen.tsx:75-82` ‚Äî `loadTemplates` defined but only called on button press; deviceId may not be loaded yet at that point
- `frontend/Rally/src/navigation/MainTabNavigator.tsx:11-12` ‚Äî `MoreScreen` and `PlayerCardDemo` imported but **never referenced** in any route definition
- `frontend/Rally/src/navigation/MainTabNavigator.tsx:295-301` ‚Äî "More" tab uses `SessionOverviewScreen` directly as component (not wrapped in a stack navigator), so it cannot push sub-screens

---

### CHANGES

None.

---

### RISKS

- **Stale-closure data loss in SessionDetailScreen**: The `initializeScreen` function copies `sessionData` to clipboard using stale state after `fetchSessionData`. For new sessions, users get no clipboard copy. Mitigation: return session from `fetchSessionData` and use the return value directly.
- **Score cap makes game completion impossible for real matches**: Scores limited to 0-2 effectively make the `updateGameScore` function unusable for actual badminton (21-point games). Mitigation: remove or raise the cap, or add a configurable max.
- **Template save broken for all iOS/Android users**: `Alert.prompt` doesn't exist on either platform, so the "Save as Template" button silently does nothing. Mitigation: replace with a custom modal containing a `TextInput`.
- **Memory leak in TrendsDashboardScreen**: If `playerId` is undefined or empty string, `setLoading(true)` fires but the `Promise.all` block is skipped, so `setLoading(false)` never runs ‚Üí infinite spinner. Mitigation: early-return with `setLoading(false)` when `playerId` is falsy.
- **7 screens hardcode API_BASE_URL**: Any environment change (staging, production) requires editing 7 files instead of 1 config. Mitigation: import from `config/api` consistently.

---

### BLOCKERS

None.

agents.result:
Now I have all the evidence needed for a comprehensive report. Let me compile the findings.

---

### SUMMARY

I performed a security, error-handling, and cross-cutting concern review across 8 areas of the Rally project (auth middleware, server config, Prisma schema, route registration, error handler, rate limiter, frontend API services, and console.log usage). The review found 1 CRITICAL issue (duplicate route registration causing confusion and potential undefined behavior), 6 MAJOR issues (including a misconfigured rate-limit path, broken analytics API base URL, and hardcoded localhost URLs across frontend services), 5 MEDIUM issues (dead MongoDB error handling in a PostgreSQL project, broken rate-limit skip logic, and CSP weaknesses), and 5 LOW issues (inconsistent logging, debug console.log statements in production paths). The MVP auth bypass is correctly gated but poorly documented.

### EVIDENCE

- `backend/src/middleware/auth.ts:20` ‚Äî MVP bypass condition: `process.env.MVP_MODE === 'true' || process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'`; `.env.example` prescribes `NODE_ENV=development`
- `backend/src/routes/index.ts:14,24` ‚Äî `notificationRoutes` and `notificationsRoutes` both imported from `'./notifications'` on lines 14 and 24
- `backend/src/routes/index.ts:71,95` ‚Äî Both mount on `router.use('/notifications', ...)` at lines 71 and 95; second shadows the first
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/analyticsApi.ts:13` ‚Äî `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api'` (missing `/v1`, no env var)
- `frontend/Rally/src/services/NotificationService.ts:222,251,276` ‚Äî Three method bodies contain `const API_BASE_URL = 'http://localhost:3001/api/v1'` hardcoded
- `frontend/Rally/src/hooks/useMessaging.ts:6` ‚Äî `const SOCKET_URL = 'http://localhost:3001'` hardcoded
- `backend/src/server.ts:90-91` ‚Äî `app.use('/api/', limiter)` mounts rate limiter on `/api/` with trailing slash; main routes are at `/api/v1`
- `backend/src/middleware/errorHandler.ts:36-51` ‚Äî Handles `CastError`, `MongoError` code 11000, `ValidationError` (all Mongoose/MongoDB specific; project uses Prisma/PostgreSQL)
- `backend/src/middleware/rateLimit.ts:130-157` ‚Äî `skipSuccessfulRequests`/`skipFailedRequests` wrap `res.json`/`res.send` but counting occurred before wrapper installation; feature is non-functional
- `backend/prisma/schema.prisma:1137-1230` ‚Äî Achievement model: `badgeId` nullable, `icon` nullable; Badge model: `icon` required (inconsistency). PlayerAchievement: no `@@index([earnedAt])` or `@@index([completedAt])`, `playerId` has no `@relation`
- `backend/src/server.ts:36-39` ‚Äî CSP allows `'unsafe-inline'` and `'unsafe-eval'` in `scriptSrc`
- `backend/src/routes/mvpSessions.ts:471-476` ‚Äî `console.log('üîç DEBUG: Session data from database:', {...})` with full session data

### CHANGES

None.

### RISKS

- The duplicate `/notifications` route registration could cause subtle bugs: if the first-registered handler has different middleware, it may still process requests before the second handler, or vice-versa depending on Express ordering. The actual behavior depends on whether the imported modules are identical ‚Äî since both import from `'./notifications'`, the module is the same singleton, so requests go through the same router twice. This means every notification endpoint handler fires twice per request, which could lead to duplicate DB writes, double notification sends, and wasted resources.
- The `analyticsApi.ts` base URL `http://localhost:3001/api` points to a path that doesn't exist (backend serves `/api/v1`). All analytics API calls from the frontend will 404 in production. This appears to be a significant undiscovered bug.
- The rate-limit middleware `app.use('/api/', limiter)` with a trailing slash may not match `/api/v1/...` paths correctly in all Express versions, potentially leaving API routes without rate limiting. Mitigation: use `app.use('/api', limiter)` without the trailing slash.
- `NODE_ENV=development` in `.env.example` combined with the auth bypass means anyone copying the example file verbatim to production gets no authentication. Mitigation: remove the `NODE_ENV` fallback from the bypass condition, or add a prominent comment in `.env.example`.

### BLOCKERS

None.

agents.result:
I now have all the data needed. Here's the complete report.

---

### SUMMARY
The Rally project has a well-scaffolded 1:1 / small-group messaging system with a full REST API, Socket.io real-time events, and polished React Native UI components. Both backend and frontend cover thread creation, message send/receive, typing indicators, read receipts, message deletion, search, and unread counts. However, the infrastructure is designed around direct message threads identified by participant lists ‚Äî it lacks group chat concepts (named rooms, admin roles, pinned messages, media attachments, @mentions, read-by receipts per participant, group avatars, or push-to-talk). The socket server handles messaging events but the central `socketService.ts` on the frontend does NOT listen for or emit any chat events ‚Äî the real-time chat logic lives entirely in a separate `useMessaging` hook with its own socket instance.

---

### EVIDENCE

- **`backend/prisma/schema.prisma:1386-1430`** ‚Äî Two models:
  - `MessageThread`: `id (String @id)`, `participants (String[])`, `title (String?)`, `lastMessageAt (DateTime)`, `isActive (Boolean)`, and relation `messages Message[]`. Maps to `message_threads`. Indexes on `participants`, `lastMessageAt`, `isActive`.
  - `Message`: `id (String @id)`, `threadId (String)`, `senderId (String)`, `content (String)`, `messageType (MessageType, default TEXT)`, `sentAt (DateTime)`, `isRead (Boolean, default false)`, `readAt (DateTime?)`. Relations to `MessageThread` (Cascade) and `MvpPlayer` (sender). Maps to `messages`. Indexes on `threadId`, `senderId`, `sentAt`, `isRead`.
  - No `Conversation`, `GroupChat`, `GroupMember`, `GroupAdmin`, `GroupMessage` models.

- **`backend/src/routes/messaging.ts:1-290`** ‚Äî 6 REST endpoints, ALL require `authenticateToken`:
  - `POST /threads` ‚Äî create thread (participants + optional title)
  - `GET /threads` ‚Äî list user's threads
  - `POST /messages` ‚Äî send message (threadId, content, messageType)
  - `GET /threads/:threadId/messages` ‚Äî paginate messages (limit/offset)
  - `POST /threads/:threadId/read` ‚Äî mark messages as read
  - `GET /unread` ‚Äî total unread count
  - `GET /threads/:threadId/unread` ‚Äî per-thread unread count
  - `POST /threads/:threadId/leave` ‚Äî leave a thread
  - `POST /threads/:threadId/participants` ‚Äî add participants
  - `DELETE /messages/:messageId` ‚Äî delete a message
  - `GET /threads/:threadId` ‚Äî thread details
  - `GET /threads/:threadId/search` ‚Äî search messages (query + limit)

- **`backend/src/services/messagingService.ts:1-290`** ‚Äî 11 methods on `MessagingService`:
  - `createThread(data)` ‚Äî deduplicates by exact participant set
  - `sendMessage(data)` ‚Äî verifies participant membership, inserts, returns with sender name
  - `getThreadMessages(threadId, userId, limit, offset)` ‚Äî paginated, chrono order
  - `getUserThreads(userId)` ‚Äî with last message preview
  - `markMessagesAsRead(threadId, userId)` ‚Äî bulk update
  - `getUnreadCount(userId)` ‚Äî aggregate count
  - `getThreadUnreadCount(threadId, userId)` ‚Äî per-thread count
  - `deleteMessage(messageId, userId)` ‚Äî physical delete (owner-only)
  - `leaveThread(threadId, userId)` ‚Äî removes from participants, deletes thread if empty
  - `addParticipants(threadId, userId, newParticipants)` ‚Äî merges participant arrays
  - `getThreadDetails(threadId, userId)` ‚Äî with message count
  - `searchMessages(threadId, userId, query, limit)` ‚Äî ILIKE search
  - All use raw SQL (`$queryRaw`), not Prisma ORM.

- **`backend/src/config/socket.ts:259-375`** ‚Äî Messaging socket events (server-side):
  - `messaging:join-thread` ‚Äî joins Socket.io room `thread-{id}`
  - `messaging:leave-thread` ‚Äî leaves room
  - `messaging:send-message` ‚Äî saves to DB via `messagingService`, broadcasts `messaging:new-message` to room
  - `messaging:typing-start` ‚Äî broadcasts `messaging:user-typing` to room (excludes sender)
  - `messaging:typing-stop` ‚Äî broadcasts `messaging:user-stopped-typing` to room
  - `messaging:mark-read` ‚Äî calls service, broadcasts `messaging:messages-read` to room
  - `messaging:delete-message` ‚Äî calls service, broadcasts `messaging:message-deleted` to room
  - Error handling: `messaging:error` emitted back to sender on failures

- **`frontend/Rally/src/screens/ConversationListScreen.tsx:1-256`** ‚Äî Fully built conversation list:
  - Pull-to-refresh, search/filter, FlatList with `ConversationCard`
  - New chat button ‚Üí navigates to FriendsList to pick a participant
  - Long-press ‚Üí delete/leave confirmation
  - Empty state with illustration and CTA
  - No group creation flow; only 1:1 via friend picker

- **`frontend/Rally/src/screens/ChatScreen.tsx:1-260`** ‚Äî Fully built chat screen:
  - Loads messages with pagination (infinite scroll up)
  - Date separators ("Today", "Yesterday", weekday, full date)
  - Real-time via `useMessaging` hook with REST fallback
  - Typing indicator display, offline banner
  - KeyboardAvoidingView, auto-scroll to bottom
  - Mark-as-read on enter
  - Uses `MessageBubble`, `MessageInput`, `TypingIndicator` sub-components

- **`frontend/Rally/src/services/messagingApi.ts:1-295`** ‚Äî 11 API methods on `MessagingApiService`:
  - `createThread(participants, title?)`, `getUserThreads()`, `getThreadDetails(threadId)`
  - `getThreadMessages(threadId, limit, offset)`, `sendMessage(threadId, content, messageType)`
  - `markAsRead(threadId)`, `getUnreadCount()`, `getThreadUnreadCount(threadId)`
  - `deleteMessage(messageId)`, `leaveThread(threadId)`, `addParticipants(threadId, participants)`
  - `searchMessages(threadId, query, limit)`
  - All use Bearer token auth from AsyncStorage

- **`frontend/Rally/src/services/socketService.ts:1-340`** ‚Äî Does NOT handle any chat events. The `SocketEvents` interface covers session, discovery, status, pairing, and game events only. No `messaging:*` events are listed, emitted, or listened to. The chat real-time logic lives in a separate `useMessaging` hook.

- **`frontend/Rally/src/hooks/useMessaging.ts:1-238`** ‚Äî Separate Socket.io instance for chat:
  - Creates its OWN `io()` connection (not using `socketService` singleton)
  - Listens to: `messaging:new-message`, `messaging:message-deleted`, `messaging:user-typing`, `messaging:user-stopped-typing`, `messaging:messages-read`, `messaging:error`
  - Emits: `messaging:send-message`, `messaging:typing-start`, `messaging:typing-stop`, `messaging:delete-message`, `messaging:mark-read`, `messaging:join-thread`, `messaging:leave-thread`
  - Auto-stop typing after 3s, `auth:identify` on connect
  - **BUG**: Uses `API_BASE_URL` without importing it (line 6: `const SOCKET_URL = API_BASE_URL;`)

- **`frontend/Rally/src/components/ConversationCard.tsx:1-176`** ‚Äî Rich card: avatar initials, online dot, relative timestamps, message preview, unread badge, chevron. Full UI.

- **`frontend/Rally/src/components/MessageBubble.tsx:1-150`** ‚Äî Own/other alignment, blue/gray bubbles, sender name on received, read receipts (‚úì/‚úì‚úì), system message style, timestamps. Supports TEXT, IMAGE, SYSTEM, CHALLENGE types but only TEXT and SYSTEM have distinct rendering.

- **`frontend/Rally/src/components/MessageInput.tsx:1-126`** ‚Äî Text input with send button, multiline, typing indicator debounce (2s), max 5000 chars, keyboard-aware. No attachment/media button.

- **`frontend/Rally/src/components/TypingIndicator.tsx:1-100`** ‚Äî Animated bouncing dots with "Alice is typing‚Ä¶" / "Alice, Bob are typing‚Ä¶" / "Alice, Bob and 3 others are typing‚Ä¶" text. Fully polished.

---

### CHANGES
None.

### RISKS
- **Dual socket connections**: `useMessaging.ts` creates its own `io()` instance separate from `socketService.ts`. This means two WebSocket connections per client when both chat and session features are active ‚Äî doubling overhead. Mitigation: consolidate into the singleton `socketService`.
- **`API_BASE_URL` not imported in `useMessaging.ts`**: Line 6 references an undefined variable, which will cause a runtime crash. Mitigation: add `import { API_BASE_URL } from '../config/api';`.
- **No media support**: `MessageType` enum includes `IMAGE` but the `MessageInput` has no attachment button and `MessageBubble` has no image rendering path. Sending images would fail silently or display as blank.
- **Physical delete**: `deleteMessage` does a hard `DELETE FROM messages` rather than soft-delete, so message history can be permanently lost.
- **No group concept**: Threads are identified solely by participant arrays ‚Äî there's no named group, no group avatar, no admin/owner role. If a 5-person thread needs to become 6, it's possible via `addParticipants`, but the UX doesn't surface this in the chat screen.

### BLOCKERS
None.
